channel.title: The Grant Desk - Writers, US
channel.description: Paid open calls, fellowships and residencies in AI, tech, research, and digital and mixed-media arts, sorted into the right pile. Updated as new calls land on the desk. Filtered to: Writers, US.

item.title: Woodward/Newman Award 2027-28 (Constellation Stage & Screen)
item.description:
Organisation: Constellation Stage & Screen

Location: Bloomington, Indiana, USA. Open submissions of unpublished plays; production takes place in Bloomington.

Award: USD 3,000 cash prize (in lieu of royalties) plus a full production in Constellation's Mainstage season; housing and transportation provided. No submission fee.

Duration: Winner announced by 15 May 2027; production in the 2027-28 season. Submissions stay on file and under consideration for two years.

Deadline: 31 Aug 2026

The Woodward/Newman Award, started with the support of Joanne Woodward, Newman's Own Foundation and the Newman family, is awarded annually by Constellation Stage & Screen to the best unpublished new play. The winner receives a USD 3,000 cash prize (in lieu of royalties) and a full production in the Mainstage season, with housing and transportation provided. The contest format and submission fees have been eliminated: submissions are reviewed on an ongoing basis and are also considered for all seven production slots in the season, including TYA (theatre for young audiences) shows. Full-length plays should run 75 to 135 minutes; TYA shows over 40 minutes. Plays must be unpublished at submission (independently published is acceptable); limit of two submissions per playwright per year; musicals may include demos. Plays submitted by midnight 31 August 2026 are considered for the 2027-28 season; later submissions roll to 2028-29. All submissions are kept on file for two years.

Open call details

Want to see more grants? Visit The Grant Desk (https://www.artificialnouveau.com/smalltools/grants/).

item.title: Ucross Fellowship for Native American Writers: Spring 2027
item.description:
Organisation: Ucross Foundation

Location: Sheridan, Wyoming, USA

Award: Fully funded fellowship residency; living accommodations, meals, and work space provided. No application fee.

Duration: Spring session (February through early June 2027); residencies range from two to six weeks

Deadline: Closed 15 Jul 2026

The Ucross Fellowship for Native American Writers is a dedicated fellowship within Ucross Foundation's residency program, offering fully funded time and space to write on a working cattle ranch in the foothills of Wyoming's Bighorn Mountains. The fellowship provides living accommodations, meals, and work space at no charge, with no application fee. One Fellowship winner is selected per session; all applicants also have the option of being considered for a general Ucross residency. ELIGIBILITY: open to Native American writers who are practicing contemporary writers currently producing work in one or more genres, including but not limited to fiction, nonfiction, poetry, drama, screenwriting, playwriting, and hybrid forms; and who are enrolled members of a state-recognized or federally-recognized Tribe, Pueblo, Nation, Native Community, Political Entity, or Alaskan Native Village. The work sample and project description are the most significant features of the application. Unless the work is genuinely interdisciplinary (the genres interconnect), applicants are encouraged to apply in a single primary discipline and submit a work sample and project description emphasizing that discipline. Work-sample requirements: FICTION, 20 pages of double-spaced fiction (a novel excerpt, a story, several stories, or a combination); NONFICTION, 20 pages of double-spaced nonfiction; POETRY, 10 pages of poetry (may be single-spaced); PLAYWRITING, one complete play (production documentation may be included if relevant), noting the 20 pages for reviewers to read; SCREENWRITING, one complete screenplay (production documentation may be included if relevant), noting the 20 pages for reviewers to read. All writing samples should include your full name. The open call for the Spring 2027 session closes July 15, 2026 (apply by 11:59 p.m. MT). A separate Ucross Fellowship for Native American Visual Artists is also offered.

Open call details

Want to see more grants? Visit The Grant Desk (https://www.artificialnouveau.com/smalltools/grants/).

item.title: The Center for Fiction / Susan Kamil Emerging Writer Fellowship
item.description:
Organisation: The Center for Fiction

Location: New York City, USA

Award: USD 5,000 grant plus writers studio space, mentorship, agent introductions, and community programming

Duration: One year (the fellowship year)

Deadline: 31 Jul 2026

The Center for Fiction / Susan Kamil Emerging Writer Fellowship annually supports nine early-career, New York City-based fiction writers at a pivotal point in their careers. Each fellow receives a 5,000 USD grant, dedicated space and time to write in the Writers Studio, personalized editorial mentorship, opportunities to meet literary agents, and community programming. Applicants must be current residents of one of the five boroughs and must remain in New York City for the entire fellowship year. An emerging writer is defined as someone who has not yet had a novel or short story collection published by a major or independent publisher and who is not currently under contract for a work of fiction; students enrolled in a degree-granting program during the fellowship year are not eligible. The 2027 fellowship application window opens July 1, 2026 and closes July 31, 2026.

Open call details

Want to see more grants? Visit The Grant Desk (https://www.artificialnouveau.com/smalltools/grants/).

item.title: imprint: a QTBIPOC Dance Writing Fellowship
item.description:
Organisation: pateldanceworks

Location: San Francisco Bay Area, California, United States

Award: USD 500 stipend per fellow, plus writing and publishing mentorship

Duration: One program year (approximately September to September)

Deadline: Closed 30 Jun 2026

imprint is an annual dance writing fellowship from pateldanceworks for emerging QTBIPOC (queer, trans, Black, Indigenous, and people of color) dance writers based in the San Francisco Bay Area. Artistic Director Bhumi B Patel supports three selected fellows with writing and publishing mentorship and professional development over a program year. Each fellow receives a USD 500 stipend and commits roughly 30 hours across six cohort meetings that include readings, discussions, writing feedback, and guest speakers. Fellows attend at least five Bay Area dance performances and have two to three completed pieces published on the pateldanceworks blog. The program is not intended for full-time students given the time requirements. There is no application fee.

Open call details

Want to see more grants? Visit The Grant Desk (https://www.artificialnouveau.com/smalltools/grants/).

item.title: Hellmouth Magazine: Volume 3 "Metamorphosis" - Call for Submissions
item.description:
Organisation: Hellmouth Magazine

Location: Knoxville, TN, USA; Southeast US focus for reviews, but national and international exceptions considered. Remote submissions.

Award: $50 honorarium upon publication. No submission fee.

Duration: One-time publication in Volume 3.

Deadline: Closed 20 Jun 2026

Hellmouth Magazine, a 501(c)(3) nonprofit arts and culture publication based in Knoxville, TN, is seeking submissions for Volume 3 on the theme of "Metamorphosis." The magazine publishes twice annually, covering experimental essays, critical art writing, reviews, interviews, and visual art. HONORARIUM: $50 upon publication. CATEGORIES OPEN: Reviews (exhibitions, gallery events, artist shows, cultural happenings -- Southeast focus but national/international exceptions welcomed; 500-750 words); Interviews (artists living or dead; video interviews also welcome; open word count); Art Writing (academic or experimental essays on contemporary or historical art -- artists, movements, zeitgeist, concepts, theories; 1,200+ words); Visual Art (digital output or print; best submitted alongside an upcoming show or event). FICTION & POETRY: closed for this volume. THEME: Metamorphosis as transformative vehicle -- post-Anthropocentric systems, chimeras, the post-human and transhuman, cultural and political shapeshifting, transformation through art and literature in the wake of collective trauma. All pitches should be approximately 500 words. Read recent issues before submitting. Submissions due 20 June 2026. Submit at https://www.hellmouthmag.com/submit.

Open call details

Want to see more grants? Visit The Grant Desk (https://www.artificialnouveau.com/smalltools/grants/).

item.title: Upstream Creative Writers / Grassroots Journalism Residency 2026: Writing About Natural Places, Stewards, and the Spaces We Care For
item.description:
Organisation: Upstream

Location: Remote residency in Minnesota, USA; applicants must be Minnesota residents living in East Side St Paul; West Central Minnesota (Becker, Clay, Douglas, Grant, Otter Tail, Pope, Stevens, Traverse, and Wilkin Counties); or the Iron Range (Aitkin, Carlton, Cook, Itasca, Koochiching, Lake, and Saint Louis Counties).

Award: USD 3,000 artist stipend, plus mileage reimbursement up to USD 500, a Minnesota State Parks Pass, mentorship and creative support, and the opportunity to publish and share work statewide. No application fee.

Duration: 9-month remote residency

Deadline: Closed 06 Jul 2026

The Upstream Creative Writers / Grassroots Journalism Residency invites creative writers, poets, storytellers and grassroots journalists to spend intentional time over nine months exploring Minnesota's parks, waterways, forests and natural places, and the people who care for them. The residency seeks writers who care about storytelling rooted in place, environment, community, stewardship and connection. SELECTED MINNESOTA ARTISTS RECEIVE: a USD 3,000 artist stipend; mileage reimbursement up to USD 500; a Minnesota State Parks Pass; mentorship and creative support; and the opportunity to publish and share their work statewide. ELIGIBILITY: applicants must be a Minnesota resident, 18 years or older, with a residence in East Side St Paul; West Central Minnesota (Becker, Clay, Douglas, Grant, Otter Tail, Pope, Stevens, Traverse, and Wilkin Counties); or the Iron Range, Minnesota (Aitkin, Carlton, Cook, Itasca, Koochiching, Lake, and Saint Louis Counties). Applications open 8 June 2026; deadline 6 July 2026 by 11:59pm. Apply via the online JotForm.

Open call details

Want to see more grants? Visit The Grant Desk (https://www.artificialnouveau.com/smalltools/grants/).

item.title: PEN America Literary Grants 2027
item.description:
Organisation: PEN America

Location: United States (US-based writers)

Award: Varies by grant (e.g. PEN/Jean Stein Grant for Literary Oral History, PEN/Phyllis Naylor Working Writer Fellowship), generally in the $5,000 to $10,000+ range. No application fee.

Duration: Project / works-in-progress support

Deadline: Closed 15 Jun 2026

PEN America's suite of literary grants and fellowships supporting individual writers and works in progress across genres (fiction, nonfiction, poetry, oral history and more), each with its own focus and criteria. ELIGIBILITY: varies by grant; generally US-based writers, with several open to writers and translators at different career stages. Distinct from the PEN/Heim Translation Fund and PEN Presents. Review individual grant criteria and apply via the PEN America literary grants page.

Open call details

Want to see more grants? Visit The Grant Desk (https://www.artificialnouveau.com/smalltools/grants/).

item.title: Alan Andres Writing Fellowships 2026-2027 (Boston Public Library)
item.description:
Organisation: Associates of the Boston Public Library

Location: Boston / New England, United States

Award: Two fellowships (apply to only one): Writer-in-Residence: $70,000 stipend plus $2,500 editorial support and a year-long office residency at the Boston Public Library. Picture Book Writer Fellowship: $25,000 stipend plus $2,500 editorial support. No application fee.

Duration: Writer-in-Residence: one year (October 2026 to September 2027)

Deadline: Closed 05 Jun 2026

The Associates of the Boston Public Library offer the Alan Andres Writing Fellowships for emerging New England writers, funded by an anonymous donor committed to writers earning a living wage. Applicants may apply to only one fellowship; winners are chosen through a blind judging process. (1) ALAN ANDRES WRITER-IN-RESIDENCE (22nd year): a year-long residency (October 2026 to September 2027) with an office at the Boston Public Library, a $70,000 stipend, and $2,500 for editorial support, to complete a manuscript for children or young adults (fiction, nonfiction, plays, poetry, or graphic novels); apply at www.writer-in-residence.org. (2) ALAN ANDRES PICTURE BOOK WRITER FELLOWSHIP (formerly the Illustrated Children's Book Fellowship, 2nd year): a $25,000 stipend plus $2,500 for editorial support to complete a manuscript for young readers (fiction, nonfiction, memoir, or poetry for ages 0-2 board books or ages 3-8 picture books and early readers); apply at www.associatesbpl.org/picturebook. ELIGIBILITY: emerging New England writers. Applications close June 5, 2026.

Open call details

Want to see more grants? Visit The Grant Desk (https://www.artificialnouveau.com/smalltools/grants/).

item.title: Kate Medina Fellowship for Literary Narrative Nonfiction 2026 (NYPL)
item.description:
Organisation: New York Public Library, in partnership with Random House

Location: New York City, USA (on-site research at the NYPL Schwarzman Building)

Award: $30,000 stipend for four continuous months of research (September 2026 to March 2027). No application fee.

Duration: Four continuous months of research

Deadline: Closed 15 Jun 2026

Fellowship from the New York Public Library and Random House for writers of literary narrative nonfiction whose projects engage NYPL's archival and special collections. ELIGIBILITY: writers of narrative nonfiction with a project requiring on-site access to the collections at the Stephen A. Schwarzman Building in New York. Apply via the NYPL fellowships page.

Open call details

Want to see more grants? Visit The Grant Desk (https://www.artificialnouveau.com/smalltools/grants/).

item.title: Letras Boricuas Fellowship 2026 (5th and final cohort)
item.description:
Organisation: Flamboyan Arts Fund, in partnership with the Mellon Foundation

Location: Puerto Rico and its diaspora (remote; writers with sustained ties to Puerto Rico)

Award: $25,000 unrestricted award to each of 24 fellows. No application fee.

Duration: One-off unrestricted fellowship

Deadline: Closed 22 May 2026

Fifth and final cohort of the Letras Boricuas Fellowship, awarding unrestricted support to writers with deep, sustained ties to Puerto Rico and its diaspora. ELIGIBILITY: emerging and established writers across fiction, creative nonfiction, poetry, spoken word and playwriting, who demonstrate a sustained connection to Puerto Rico and its diaspora. Apply via the Flamboyan Arts Fund portal.

Open call details

Want to see more grants? Visit The Grant Desk (https://www.artificialnouveau.com/smalltools/grants/).

item.title: Whiting Foundation: Nonfiction Grant for Works-in-Progress 2026
item.description:
Organisation: Whiting Foundation

Location: Canada, UK, or US (project must be under contract with a publisher in one of these three countries)

Award: $40,000 to each of ten writers ($400,000 total).

Duration: Per project (mid-process stage of a multiyear book project)

Deadline: Closed 31 May 2026

Annual grant from the Whiting Foundation supporting writers completing deeply researched, imaginatively composed book-length nonfiction for a general adult readership. Ten grants of $40,000 each, intended for the mid-process stage of multiyear projects after substantial progress but before the final work is complete. Eligible categories include history, cultural or political reportage, biography, memoir, science, philosophy, criticism, graphic nonfiction, and personal essays. Excluded: self-help, historical fiction, textbooks, books for a scholarly audience, books for young readers, and self-published projects. Hard eligibility constraint: project must be under contract with a publisher in Canada, the UK, or the US by 31 May 2026, with a fully executed contract uploaded; no extensions are granted for contracts not signed by both parties by the application deadline. Application includes the original proposal, up to 15,000 words from the draft, statement of work yet to be completed, plan for use of funds, three written responses on premise/research methods/narrative approach, signed contract, 2-4 page resume, list of prior funding for the book, and a required letter of support from the publisher (plus optional letters of recommendation). Free to apply. Grantees announced December 2026 / January 2027.

Open call details

Want to see more grants? Visit The Grant Desk (https://www.artificialnouveau.com/smalltools/grants/).
